meta.description: Configuration-driven registry of official government sources for US data center and AI policy tracking. Each entry records the jurisdiction, document type, URL, tier, adapter type, and verification status. The pipeline reads this file to know what to check and how to fetch it. Candidates discovered via this registry go into policy_candidates.json — NOT directly into restrictions_raw.json.
meta.url_verified_note: url_verified=false means the URL has not been live-checked in this session. The pipeline validates URLs on each run and updates source_health.json. All URLs are drawn from: (a) existing restrictions_raw.json source objects, (b) well-documented state legislature domains, or (c) official agency domains consistent with government URL conventions. No URLs are invented.

tiers.1: Official government source (.gov, legislature, official agency)
tiers.2: Reputable code publisher or official utility/industry body
tiers.3: News or discovery only — never authoritative

adapters.generic_html: Fetch HTML page and keyword-scan for policy signals
adapters.rss_atom: Parse RSS or Atom feed for agenda/document updates
adapters.sitemap: Parse XML sitemap to discover new ordinance/zoning pages
adapters.legistar: Legistar legislative management API (used by many city/county councils)
adapters.state_legislature: State legislature search or RSS API
adapters.open_data: Open data portal API (CKAN, Socrata, data.gov)

sources.title: Arizona Legislature — Bill Search and Status

sources.notes: Tracks 100-year assured water supply requirements affecting data center permitting in AZ. URL from restrictions_raw.json.
